title: Bulletproof Gemini routing and smoother local dev
summary:
A day of quiet optimizations: bulletproof Gemini routing, smoother OAuth testing, UI layout fixes, and a bundle-shrinking return to pure ASCII.

We smoothed out a couple of developer friction points today. Gemini-3-flash requests now route through api.airforce by default, with a seamless Portkey failover to GCP Vertex if things get weird. Same model, same billing, fewer dropped requests. On the frontend, new app keys now pre-fill a localhost callback so you can test OAuth flows immediately without wrestling with allowlists.

The model catalog UI got a quick fix to stop category tabs from jumping around while data loads. Meanwhile, the Sirius Cybernetics Elevator Challenge app shed its background images and animations, returning to its original, distraction-free dark-terminal ASCII roots. As a bonus, the bundle size dropped from 601kB to a breezy 170kB.
